content: We talked about this ages back but I want to bring it up again. Personally I feel like it's bad etiquette to auto-subscribe everyone to new streams. From what I remember last time this was discussed, I'm basically the only person with that opinion, everyone else thinks the opposite. I guess I'm bring it up because a bunch of new streams have been created recently
I'm super aggressive about unsubbing from streams that other people subbed me to. But I can't think of any other platform where the equivalent of a stream is opt-out rather than opt-in
Maybe if there were a weekly thread where a bot could post the new streams... but maybe that is too much work to implement

New streams are already automatically announced by a built-in bot in #general > new streams. But experience shows that people don't pay attention to it, and new streams generally become ghost towns unless you pull in 1000 people against their will]

I think it's super-important to auto-subscribe people to new streams, because otherwise most people won't even know what is going on here. And I think almost everyone agrees. If new streams were created almost daily it would become a nuisance to unsubscribe to them. But we shouldn't let that happen; we don't need zillions of streams.
Yup, I accept that I'm in the minority opinion here. I just wanted to bring it up again
The number of new streams being created should fall over time too, so this will become less and less of an issue.

(As an arbitrary personal restriction, I try to always keep the left hand bar entirely on the screen without having to scroll, even when one of them is expanded to show its recent topics. That means that I can always see at a glance where there are new messages)
By the way, does this left hand bar show all streams, or just streams you're subscribed to? (For me those two are the same.)

Yeah I guess I'm already subscribed to an overwhelming number of streams...so I'm disappointed when there's a cool stream that I never knew about. I just recently went through and added all the streams I wanted to see.
Here's my sidebar, which is the same as the list of all streams I'm subbed to: Screenshot-2021-01-28-at-16.53.53.png

I'm subscribed to all streams that I can find, because I'm trying to help build up this community, so I'm curious to see what people are talking about, even if it's applications of intuitionistic type theory internal to an arbitrary symmetric monoidal bicategory to shoe repair, in Portuguese.
There's a bunch of pretty active streams that I unsubbed from because it felt like a distraction

But I don't want to say everyone should subscribe to all possible streams! I hope the creation of new streams happens rarely enough that people don't need to spend lots of time unsubscribing from them.
I think ideally, when a new stream is created, everyone is auto-enrolled, and then people can choose to leave if its not interesting to them.

If you're trying to limit the number of streams you subscribe to you might not know the following: if you're subscribed to a lot of streams, inactive ones get greyed out and moved to the bottom of the list. I'm subscribed to most of the streams, and the active ones only take up about a screenful on the side-bar, so all the inactive ones don't really bother me.
